title: Soy candle — sea salt and driftwood
subtitle: A clean, aquatic soy wax container candle scented with sea salt and driftwood fragrance oil
excerpt: Sea salt and driftwood fragrance oil gives a clean, aquatic scent with a light woody base note. This is a cooler, less sweet fragrance than florals or vanillas, suited to bathrooms and kitchens. The soy wax formula and method are the same as for any container candle; the scent choice makes it feel coastal and fresh.
sourceNotes: Soy wax container candle method: Bramble Berry candle-making guide (2023). Sea salt and driftwood fragrance oil: aquatic-woody fragrance family; good performance in soy wax. Fragrance load 7% of wax weight. Pour temperature 55 to 60°C.

recipe.yieldDescription: 1 candle (200 ml glass jar, ~150 g finished wax)
recipe.batchNotes: Scale wax and fragrance proportionally for multiple jars.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: Cure for at least 3 days before the first burn.
recipe.shelfLifeNotes: Two years away from direct sunlight. Fragrance fades after around two years; wax is stable indefinitely.

glossaryTerms.definition: The percentage of fragrance oil by wax weight. At 7%, this candle has a clear cold and hot throw without the fragrance pooling on the surface.
The first burn. Allow the melt pool to reach the jar edge to prevent tunnelling on subsequent burns.
When a candle burns down the centre without melting the edges, leaving a ring of unburned wax. Caused by extinguishing before the first melt pool reaches the jar edge.

content.text: Sea salt and driftwood fragrance oil sits in the aquatic family: clean, slightly mineral, with a light woody backing. It performs well in soy wax without acceleration or discolouration. A 7%
gives a noticeable but not heavy scent. This candle suits bathrooms, kitchens, and any room that benefits from a cooler, less sweet fragrance profile.
Melt wax over a simmering-water bath on low heat only. Never leave melting wax unattended. Keep a kitchen-fire blanket within reach.

items.prepNote: 7% of wax weight; add at 55 to 60°C
Pre-tabbed cotton wick for a 65 mm jar

content.text: Wick, melt, add fragrance, and pour

Centre the pre-tabbed wick in the jar. Melt soy wax in the candle pitcher over a simmering-water bath to 65°C. Remove from heat and cool to 55 to 60°C. Add the fragrance oil and stir for 2 minutes. Pour slowly into the wicked jar. Tap gently to release air bubbles.
Leave undisturbed for 3 to 5 days. Trim the wick to 5 mm before the first burn.
. Burn for 3 to 4 hours until the melt pool reaches the jar edge. Extinguishing early causes
Two years away from direct sunlight. Store with a lid or cover to keep dust off the wax surface.
